zh.lifeSummary: 當Stephen Crichton of Cairns - Sheriff of Linlithgow, Lord Erskine於1350年出生在蘇格蘭艾丁波時，他的父親Edward Crichton - 2nd Laird of Sanquar25歲，母親Lady Christian Kerr - Lady of Sanquhar20歲。他於1380年與Catherine Cairns在英國蘇格蘭艾丁波結婚。他們至少是6個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他於1432年在蘇格蘭Linlithgowshire林利斯格福被列為office of sheriff of linlithgow。他去世於1433年12月12日在蘇格蘭米德洛迪安克萊頓享年83歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Stephen Crichton of Cairns - Sheriff of Linlithgow, Lord Erskine于1350年出生在苏格兰艾丁波时，他的父亲Edward Crichton - 2nd Laird of Sanquar25岁，母亲Lady Christian Kerr - Lady of Sanquhar20岁。他于1380年与Catherine Cairns在英国苏格兰艾丁波结婚。他们至少是6个儿子和1个女儿的父母。他于1432年在苏格兰Linlithgowshire林利斯格福被列为office of sheriff of linlithgow。他去世于1433年12月12日在苏格兰米德洛迪安克萊頓享年83岁。

nameMeanings.name: Crichton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish: variant of   Creighton   1.

variants.name: Creighton
meanings: Scottish and Irish: habitational name from Crichton, near Edinburgh, first recorded c. 1128 in the form Crectune, in 1287 as Crecton, and in 1360 as Creychtona. The name is probably an early hybrid compound of Old Welsh creic ‘rock’ + Older Scots tun ‘farm, settlement’ (Old English tūn). In Ireland and Britain, this form of the name is now found chiefly in northern Ireland; the more usual Scottish forms are Crichton and Crighton .
Irish: sometimes used for Gaelic Ó Creacháin or Ó Criocháin (see Crehan ).
English: habitational name from Creighton in Staffordshire, named with Celtic creig ‘rock’ + Old English tūn ‘settlement’.

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Cheng, Jing, Kam Fung, Wai Shing, Wing, Yali, Yuk.
Chinese:
variant Romanization of the surnames 歐 and 區, see   Ou   1 and 2.

variants.name: Ou
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ying, Ching, Heng, Hong, Jia, Kuang Yu, Li-Ching, Cho, Chong, Chung, Sa.  Southeast Asian Lichun, Ming, Quan, Roeun, Sokhom, Chau, Hai, Leang, Ly, Nhanh, Phan, Pho, Tha.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 歐, meaning ‘to sing someone's praise’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from the placename Dong Ou (東甌), a fief (in present-day Wenzhou in Zhejiang province) which was granted to Yao in 192  BC . It is said that Yao was a descendant of Wujiang (reigned 355–333  BC ), king of the state of Yue (located mainly in present-day Zhejiang and Jiangxi provinces) during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (ii) from the placename Ou Yu (歐餘), the name of a fief (located in present-day Zhejiang province) that was granted to Ti, the second son of Wujiang (reigned 355–333  BC ), king of the state of Yue (located mainly in present-day Zhejiang and Jiangxi provinces) during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ).

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 區, originally denoting an ancient vessel for measurement in ancient Chinese: from the personal name of Ou Zhizi (歐治子), an expert sword-maker who lived during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476 BC ). Some of his descendants inherited Ou (歐) as their surname, simplifying the Chinese character 歐 to 區.

nameMeanings.name: Cairns
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish (southern): habitational name from the lands of Cairns in Mid Calder (Midlothian).
Irish (Down, Antrim, and Tyrone): in the north of Ireland, there may have been confusion between the Scottish surname and Irish Ó Ciaráin, Anglicized as Kieran and Kearns .

variants.name: Cairnes
meanings: Scottish: variant of   Cairns  .

variants.name: Carns
meanings: Scottish: variant of   Cairns  .

variants.name: Charnock
meanings: English (Lancashire and Yorkshire): habitational name from Charnock Richard or Heath Charnock in southern Lancashire, which are probably named with a derivative of Celtic carn ‘cairn, pile of stones’ (see Cairns ).
Americanized form of Slovak Černok, a variant of Černík (see Cernik ).

variants.name: Kearns
meanings: Irish:
Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Céirín ‘descendant of Céirín’, a personal name from a diminutive of ciar ‘dark, black’, with the addition of English excrescent -s.
in Ulster, possibly a variant of   Cairns  .

variants.name: Carnes
meanings: English and Scottish: variant of Cairns or sometimes Carins, a variant of Caron , with excrescent -s.
English and Cornish: in southern England a variant of Carn , with excrescent -s.
Irish: variant of   Kearns  .

variants.name: Kieran
meanings: Irish (Louth and Monaghan): Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Ciaráin ‘descendant of Ciarán’, a byname from a diminutive of ciar ‘dark, black-haired’. Variants Ó Cearáin and Ó Céirín exist in Irish, see also Kearns . The personal name Ciarán was borne by two Irish saints, a hermit of the 5th century and the founder of the monastery at Clonmacnoise (died 547).

nameMeanings.name: Stephen
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Usual English spelling of the name of the first Christian martyr (Acts 6–7), whose feast is accordingly celebrated next after Christ's own ( 26 December ). His name is derived from the Greek word stephanos ‘garland, crown’.

variants.name: Stephan
meanings: Variant of   Stephen  , preserving the vowels of the Greek name.

variants.name: Steven
meanings: Variant of   Stephen  , reflecting the normal pronunciation of the name in the English-speaking world.

en.lifeSummary: When Stephen Crichton of Cairns - Sheriff of Linlithgow, Lord Erskine was born in 1350, in Edinburgh, Scotland, his father, Edward Crichton - 2nd Laird of Sanquar, was 25 and his mother, Lady Christian Kerr - Lady of Sanquhar, was 20. He married Catherine Cairns in 1380, in Edinburgh, Scotland, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 6 sons and 1 daughter. In 1432, at the age of 82, his occupation is listed as office of sheriff of linlithgow in Linlithgow, Linlithgowshire, Scotland. He died on 12 December 1433, in Crichton, Midlothian, Scotland, at the age of 83.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Stephen Crichton of Cairns - Sheriff of Linlithgow, Lord Erskine est né en 1350, à Edinburgh, Écosse, son père, Edward Crichton - 2nd Laird of Sanquar, avait 25 ans et sa mère, Lady Christian Kerr - Lady of Sanquhar, avait 20 ans. Il a épousé Catherine Cairns en 1380, à Edinburgh, Écosse, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 6 fils et 1 fille. En 1432, à l’âge de 82 ans, sa profession de son indiquée est office of sheriff of linlithgow à Linlithgow, Linlithgowshire, Écosse. Il est décédé le 12 décembre 1433, à Crichton, Midlothian, Écosse, à l’âge de 83 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Stephen Crichton of Cairns - Sheriff of Linlithgow, Lord Erskine 1350 in Edinburgh, Scotland geboren wurde, war sein Vater Edward Crichton - 2nd Laird of Sanquar 25 und seine Mutter Lady Christian Kerr - Lady of Sanquhar 20. Er hat 1380 in Edinburgh, Schottland, United Kingdom Catherine Cairns geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 6 Söhne und 1 Tochter. 1432, als er 82 Jahre alt war, war sein Beruf als office of sheriff of linlithgow in Linlithgow, Linlithgowshire, Scotland aufgeführt. Er starb am 12. Dezember 1433 im Alter von 83 Jahren in Crichton, Midlothian, Scotland.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Stephen Crichton of Cairns - Sheriff of Linlithgow, Lord Erskine è nato il nel 1350 nel Edinburgh, Scotland, il suo padre, Edward Crichton - 2nd Laird of Sanquar, aveva 25 anni e il suo madre, Lady Christian Kerr - Lady of Sanquhar, aveva 20 anni. Lui sposato Catherine Cairns nel 1380, nel Edinburgh, Scozia, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 6 figli e 1 figlia. Nel 1432, all'età di 82 anni, la sua occupazione è indicata come office of sheriff of linlithgow nel Linlithgow, Linlithgowshire, Scotland. Morì il 12 dicembre 1433, nel Crichton, Midlothian, Scotland, all'età di 83 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Stephen Crichton of Cairns - Sheriff of Linlithgow, Lord Erskineが1350年にスコットランドエディンブルグで生まれた時，父Edward Crichton - 2nd Laird of Sanquarは25歳，母Lady Christian Kerr - Lady of Sanquharは20歳であった。1380年にイギリススコットランドエディンブルグでCatherine Cairnsと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも6人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。1432年，82歳の時，スコットランドLinlithgowshireリンリスゴウでの職業はoffice of sheriff of linlithgowと記録されている。彼は1433年12月12日にスコットランドミドロスアンクリチトンで83歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Stephen Crichton of Cairns - Sheriff of Linlithgow, Lord Erskine(이)가 1350년 스컷랜드 에딘버그h에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Edward Crichton - 2nd Laird of Sanquar(은)는 25세였고, 그의 어머니 Lady Christian Kerr - Lady of Sanquhar(은)는 20세였다. 그는 1380년 영국 스코틀랜드 에딘버그h에서 Catherine Cairns(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여섯 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 1432년, 82세의 나이로 그의 직업은 스컷랜드 Linlithgowshire 린리스고에서 office of sheriff of linlithgow이었다. 그는 1433년 12월 12일 스컷랜드 미들로스이언 크리ch턴에서 83세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Stephen Crichton of Cairns - Sheriff of Linlithgow, Lord Erskine nasceu em 1350, em Edinburgh, Scotland, seu pai, Edward Crichton - 2nd Laird of Sanquar, tinha 25 anos e sua mãe, Lady Christian Kerr - Lady of Sanquhar, tinha 20 anos. Ele casou-se com Catherine Cairns em 1380, em Edinburgh, Escócia, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 6 filhos e 1 filha. Em 1432, com 82 anos, sua profissão aparece como office of sheriff of linlithgow em Linlithgow, Linlithgowshire, Scotland. Ele faleceu em 12 de dezembro de 1433, em Crichton, Midlothian, Scotland, com 83 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Stephen Crichton of Cairns - Sheriff of Linlithgow, Lord Erskine родился в 1350 г. в местности эдинбург, Шотландия, его отцу по имени Edward Crichton - 2nd Laird of Sanquar было 25 лет, а матери, которую звали Lady Christian Kerr - Lady of Sanquhar, 20. Он женился на женщине по имени Catherine Cairns в 1380 г. в местности эдинбург, Шотландия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 6 сыновей и 1 дочь. В 1432 году, в возрасте 82 лет,  он работал по профессии office of sheriff of linlithgow в местности линьлитгоу, Linlithgowshire, Шотландия. Он умер 12 декабря 1433 г. в местности кричтон, мидлотян, Шотландия в возрасте 83 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Stephen Crichton of Cairns - Sheriff of Linlithgow, Lord Erskine nació en 1350, en Edinburgh, Scotland, su papá, Edward Crichton - 2nd Laird of Sanquar, tenía 25 años y su mamá, Lady Christian Kerr - Lady of Sanquhar, tenía 20 años. Se casó con Catherine Cairns en 1380, en Edinburgh, Escocia, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 6 hijos y 1 hija. En 1432, a la edad de 82 años, su ocupación registrada era office of sheriff of linlithgow en Linlithgow, Linlithgowshire, Scotland. Murió el 12 de diciembre de 1433, en Crichton, Midlothian, Scotland, a la edad de 83 años.
